expressions.english: to buy clothes
expressions.notes: 옷 = clothes, outfit

expressions.english: to treat someone to a meal
expressions.notes: 밥 = food; meal; cooked rice

expressions.english: to buy something for someone and bring it to that person
expressions.notes: 주다 = to give

expressions.english: to buy something and keep it for later use
expressions.notes: 놓다 = to place, put; to let go

expressions.english: I bought it for a low price.
expressions.notes:
싸다 = to be cheap
싸게 is the adverb form of 싸다.
싸게 사다 = to buy something for a low price

expressions.english: What are you going to buy?
expressions.notes:
뭐 = what; something
뭐 is the contracted form of 무엇.
-(으)ㄹ 것이다 = will; to be going to + V

expressions.english: How much did you pay for it?
expressions.notes:
얼마 = how much
(price)에 사다 = to buy at + (price)

expressions.english: Where did you buy it?
expressions.notes: 어디 = where; somewhere

expressions.english: I want to buy this.
expressions.notes:
이거 = this thing; this
-고 싶다 = to want to + V

expressions.english: Could you buy me some medicine and bring it to me?
expressions.notes:
약 = pill; medicine
좀 = a little; please
-(으)세요 = (polite) imperative

expressions.english: When are you going to treat me to a meal?
expressions.notes:
언제 = when
밥을 사다 = to treat someone to a meal

expressions.english: Don't buy it here.
expressions.notes:
여기서 = here, from here
-지 마세요. = (polite) Don't + V.

expressions.english: If you buy this, I'll give you another one for free.
expressions.notes:
-(으)면 = if + S + V; when/once + S + V
하나 = one (native Korean number)
더 = more
드리다 = (honorific) to give

expressions.english: If I buy this, I won't have any money left.
expressions.notes:
-고 나면 = once + S + finish + V-ing
돈 = money
없다 = to not have, not exist; to be not there

expressions.english: I didn't like it, so I didn't buy it.
expressions.notes:
마음 = heart; mind
마음에 들다 = to like
안 = not
-이/이/어서 = because, since

expressions.english: I regretted buying those clothes.
expressions.notes:
그 = the; that
-고 나서 = after + S + V
후회하다 = to regret

expressions.english: This was my first time buying clothes on the Internet.
expressions.notes:
인터넷 = the Internet
처음 = first; for the first time
-이/이/어 보다 = to try V-ing

expressions.english: I was debating whether to buy it or not, and I ended up not buying it.
expressions.notes:
-(으)ㄹ까 말까 = whether to + V
고민하다 = to debate; to try to decide; to worry
-다가 = as a result of V-ing

expressions.korean: 할인 기간일 때 많이 사 놔야 해요.
expressions.english: You should buy a lot of things during the discount period. While the sale is going on, you should buy a lot of stuff (so you can use it later.)
expressions.notes:
할인 = sale, discount
기간 = period (of time), term
-(으)ㄹ 때 = while/when + S + V
많다 = to be a lot
많이 is an adverb form of 많다.
사 놓다 = to buy something (and keep it for later use)
-이/이/어야 하다 = should; have to; must

expressions.korean: 친구 선물로 뭐 살지 고민 중이에요.
expressions.english: I'm thinking about what to buy as a present for my friend.
expressions.notes:
친구 = friend
선물 = gift, present
뭐-(으)로지 고민하다 = to try to decide what to + V
중이다 = to be V-ing; to be between/middle/in the middle of V-ing
